heading: Definitions
label: 401 Definitions
notes: Regulations, , , provided that:Pub. L. 104–331, § 2(b)110 Stat. 4068Oct. 26, 1996“Appropriate measures shall be taken to ensure that—“(1)any regulations required to implement , United States Code, shall be in effect by ; andsection 411 of title 3October 1, 1997“(2)any other regulations needed to implement chapter 5 of title 3, United States Code, shall be in effect as soon as practicable, but not later than .”October 1, 1998

Applicability of Future Employment Laws, , , provided that:Pub. L. 104–331, § 4110 Stat. 4071Oct. 26, 1996“(a).—In GeneralEach provision of Federal law that is made applicable to the legislative branch under section 102 of the Congressional Accountability Act of 1995 (), and that is enacted later than 12 months after the date of the enactment of this Act [], shall be deemed to apply with respect to ‘employing offices’ and ‘covered employees’ (within the meaning of , United States Code, as added by this Act), unless such law specifically provides otherwise and expressly cites this section.2 U.S.C. 1302section 401 of title 3Oct. 26, 1996“(b)Regulations.—“(1).—In generalThe President, or the designee of the President, shall issue regulations to implement such provision.“(2).—Agency regulationsThe regulations issued under paragraph (1) to implement a provision shall be the same as substantive regulations promulgated by the head of the appropriate executive agency to implement the provision, except to the extent that the President or designee may determine, for good cause shown and stated together with the regulation, that a modification of such regulations would be more effective for the implementation of the rights and protections under the section.”

children.heading: .—Board
children.label: 1 .—Board
children.text: The term “Board” means the Merit Systems Protection Board under chapter 12 of title 5.

children.heading: .—Covered employee
children.label: 2 .—Covered employee
children.text: The term “covered employee” means any employee of an employing office.

children.heading: .—Employee
children.label: 3 .—Employee
children.text: The term “employee” includes an applicant for employment and a former employee.

children.label: A
children.text: each office, agency, or other component of the Executive Office of the President;

children.label: B
children.text: the Executive Residence at the White House; and

children.label: C
children.text: the official residence (temporary or otherwise) of the Vice President.
to which section 411 relates, the terms “employing office” and “covered employee” shall each be considered to have the meaning given to the term by such section;
to which section 412 relates, the term “covered employee” means a covered employee described in section 412(a)(2)(B);
to which section 413 relates, the term “covered employee” excludes interns and volunteers, as described in section 413(a)(2); and
to which section 416 relates, the term “covered employee” means a covered employee described in section 416(a)(2).
